workflow.name: Generate and qualify B2B leads from Telegram with Google Maps, Serper, GPT‑4o, and Gmail
workflow.description:
## How it works
- Send a target niche and location via Telegram message
- Workflow discovers businesses via Google Maps API
- AI enriches contacts with email and LinkedIn data via Serper
- GPT-4o scores and qualifies each lead
- Personalized cold email sent automatically with follow-ups

## Set up steps
- Import the workflow JSON into n8n (5 min)
- Add credentials: OpenAI, Google Maps API, Serper, Gmail, Telegram
- Configure the target niche in the settings node
- Send a test message to your Telegram bot

## Requirements
- n8n instance (cloud or self-hosted)
- OpenAI API key
- Google Maps API key
- Serper key
- Telegram bot token

workflow.name: AI Lead Gen Production Full Workflow_production

parameters.content:
## AI Lead Gen Production Full Workflow_production

### How it works

1. Receives input from Telegram and builds a query for Google Maps.
2. Searches Google Maps and processes results with a focus on duplicates and pagination.
3. Queries additional information using external searches and merges data.
4. Validates and scores potential leads.
5. Generates and sends emails with a follow-up process that includes reporting.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Set up Telegram credentials
- [ ] Configure Google Maps API Key
- [ ] Provide OpenAI API Key
- [ ] Set up Google Sheets access for appending
- [ ] Configure Gmail account for sending emails

### Customization

Adjust the scoring algorithm within the 'Lead Scoring' node to fit specific business requirements.

## Trigger and query building

Initial trigger from Telegram and builds a query for Google Maps API.

## Google Maps search

Handles search requests to Google Maps including handling pagination and duplicate removal.

## Information enrichment

Prepares keys and performs external API searches for additional data like emails and LinkedIn profiles.

## Data merging and editing

Merges data from different sources and sets fields for further processing.

## Data cleaning and validation

Cleans JSON data, validates contacts and scores leads for further action.

## Conditional lead processing

Evaluates lead score and branches for further processing, including sheet appending or email generation.

## Email sending and waiting

Handles email sending through Gmail and waits for a response period before generating follow-ups.

## Follow-up and report

Generates and sends follow-up emails, then reports back to Telegram.

values.content:
=Write a very short follow-up email (max 50 words) to {{ $node["JSON Cleaner"].json.company_name }}. 

Context: I sent them an email 3 days ago about n8n automation for {{ $node["JSON Cleaner"].json.industry }}.

Guidelines:
1. Subject: Re: Question about {{ $node["JSON Cleaner"].json.industry }} automation
2. Message: "Just checking if you saw my previous email. I'd love to hear your thoughts on those n8n ideas for {{ $node["JSON Cleaner"].json.company_name }} when you have a moment."
3. Tone: Friendly reminder, max 50 words. Language: English.

parameters.text:
=🚀 TARGET ACQUIRED! 🏢 Company: {{ $('Score Lead Data').item.json.company_name }}
💎 Industry: {{ $('Score Lead Data').item.json.industry }}
📊 Score: {{ $('Score Lead Data').item.json.lead_score }}/10
📝 Operation status:
✅ Initial email draft created in Gmail
✅ Follow-up scheduled in 3 days
✅ Data saved to Google Sheets
🕵️‍♂️ Lead hunt continues...

user.name: Yevhenii
user.bio: AI automation specialist building practical n8n workflows for business. Background in archaeology and professional translation — I bring structured, detail-oriented thinking to every automation project. Currently building a modular Restaurant AI Suite and publishing ready-to-use workflow templates.

data.details: The Function node allows you to execute JavaScript in your workflow. Unlike the Function Item node, this node does not operate on incoming node data per-item. Instead, you must iterate over multiple items of incoming data yourself. This can be useful if you're performing data transformation where you want to manipulate the number of items being outputted by the node (i.e. 1 item is inputted in with nested object, 10 items are outputted without any nested objects)

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
